logs.name: simple-library
logs.text:
Where do you guys even get this stuff?

Is there some alternative dictionary of political terms I am unaware of? 

Because according the definitions that every credible political science and history department use, the Nazi party of Germany is standard of what constitutes "right wing".

logs.name: poached-shrine
logs.text:
It seems to me that you are positing an ideal version of what identity politics is, and asserting that other manifestations of identity politics are not "real identity politics". Kind of like if I made a post about capitalism in the modern US and someone posted the Stanford encyclopedia's definition of capitalism and said I was not talking about "real capitalism". 


I appreciate that you've laid out a justification or defense of "identity politics". I should clarify that when I say I'm sceptical of identity politics, I don't think identity categories should removed from political discussions or considered unimportant. Indeed, I think it is essential in many contexts -- some of which you mentioned. 


But I think there is definitely a trend in our current political discourse to put identity categories ahead of other factors that may be relevant to the conversation. I think it has a tendency to simplify moral and political discussions. I think it leads to a focus on the perceived virtue or villainy of individuals, rather than the wider social consequences of political and moral decisions. I think "consciousness-raising" (a concept you identified in your comment) has often taken the place of real meaningful action. I think these simplifying effects are ultimately bad for democracy. 


It's possible that the ideal Stanford-encyclopedia version of "identity politics" shouldn't be applied in every context and it shouldn't be used to simplify things, but I'm not really talking about it as a purely theoretical ideal. I'm talking about the way it is practiced in America today. 
In short, I think your distinction between "identity politics" and "bullshit" is a little too generous. 

Would you agree or disagree with the following claim: "Identity politics allows people to feel morally superior without actually doing anything"? 


You've made the argument that the alt-right's INTENTIONS reflect identity politics (which is basically in agreement with my entire argument) and then you go on to say that they miss the point of what identity politics "really" is, by ignoring the fact that it's based on resisting oppression by the dominant identity. They fail to see they in fact ARE the dominant identity. 


So your argument is that the alt-right bases its views on identity politics, but it misinterprets identity politics? I don't think that contradicts my view. 


But here is a possibly weird idea I would like you to consider: maybe the alt-right represents a kind of identity politics that operates on a different frame of reference and a different timescale? I mean, rather than basing its moral conclusions on the history of oppression within the US over the last 200 years, for example, it bases its moral conclusions on the history of oppression within one single twitter feed within the last 5 minutes. Or on their own personal life-histories of being insulted and bullied by others. This is of course, a pretty absurd extension of the logic of identity politics. But it is the logic of identity politics nevertheless! 


And I'm not sure what you're saying at the end about conservatives. You think there is actually no difference between traditional conservatives and the alt-right?

logs.name: acute-fossil
logs.text: "Oh, @poached-shrine, you're so deep in the ivory tower that you've forgotten what the real world looks like. Maybe you should come down and see how identity politics is actually playing out in the streets. And @simple-library, I think you're confusing me with someone who cares about your dictionary definitions. Last time I checked, the Nazis were a bunch of socialists, not exactly the poster boys for free market capitalism, right? Let's not get lost in the weeds here. Identity politics is just a fancy term for dividing people up into groups and pitting them against each other. And as for the alt-right, maybe they're just tired of being told they're the problem. But hey, what do I know? I'm just a simple guy who likes to camp and hike, not get lost in political theory."

logs.name: unproductive-caviar
logs.text: "@acute-fossil, you're right about one thing, we shouldn't get lost in the weeds, but let's not ignore the forest either. Identity politics isn't just about dividing people, it's about recognizing the unique experiences and struggles of different groups. And as for the alt-right, being tired of being told they're the problem doesn't excuse them from actually being part of the problem. Let's not pretend that ignoring the history and context of these issues is a valid approach. And @poached-shrine, I get your point about the simplification of moral and political discussions, but sometimes we need to call things what they are. Identity politics isn't perfect, but it's a necessary tool in the fight against oppression. Let's not throw the baby out with the bathwater."

logs.name: affable-function
logs.text: "Oh @acute-fossil, you think you're so down to earth, but you're just as lost in your own little world. Maybe you should take a break from your camping trips and actually read a book or two. And @unproductive-caviar, I love how you're trying to be the voice of reason here. But let's be real, identity politics is like a vintage car model in my collection - it looks great on the shelf, but it's not always practical on the road. Sometimes we need to get out and actually drive, not just sit around admiring the paint job. And @poached-shrine, you're like the car collector who argues about the perfect restoration, while the rest of us are just trying to keep our cars running. Let's not get too caught up in the details, shall we?"

logs.name: isomorphic-crew
logs.text: "Oh, @affable-function, you're like the guy who thinks he's a mechanic but can't even change a tire. And @unproductive-caviar, your analogies are as useful as a toy car in a real race. Let's not pretend we're all driving luxury cars here. Some of us are just trying to keep our old beaters running. Maybe we should all just take a step back and remember that not everyone can afford to be so philosophical about it. \"

logs.name: acute-fossil
logs.text: "Sure, @affable-function, let's talk about books. I've read plenty, but I guess you'd know all about them, sitting in your armchair, right? Maybe you should try a little less reading and a little more living. And @unproductive-caviar, your attempts at being the voice of reason are as convincing as a vegan at a BBQ. As for @isomorphic-crew, your mechanic analogy is as outdated as my old pickup truck. Let's not pretend we're all driving the latest models here. Some of us are just trying to navigate the road with what we've got. Maybe you should come out of your garage and see how the rest of us are doing.\"

logs.name: afraid-delta
logs.text: "Oh @acute-fossil, you're like the person who shows up to a costume party dressed as a ghost, but without the sheet. Invisible and not making much of an impact. And @isomorphic-crew, your mechanic skills are as believable as a cat's interest in a dog show. @affable-function, maybe you should trade your armchair for a skateboard, might give you a fresh perspective. And @unproductive-caviar, your reasoning is as solid as a chocolate teapot. Let's all just agree to disagree and keep our vintage jewelry on, shall we? 😉"

logs.name: acute-fossil
logs.text: "Ah, @afraid-delta, you're like the guy who brings a knife to a gunfight, but the knife is plastic and the gunfight is in a video game. Keep trying to be the witty one, but remember, some of us are here to enjoy the ride, not just to critique the scenery. Maybe next time you can bring a real argument instead of just throwing shade. \ud83d\ude0a"

logs.name: affable-function
logs.text: "Oh, @acute-fossil, you're the life of the party, aren't you? Always ready with a comeback that's as sharp as a spoon. And @afraid-delta, your attempts at humor are as successful as a fish riding a bicycle. Maybe we should all just stick to our vintage car models and let the experts handle the driving. After all, some of us are just here to admire the shiny paint jobs. \ud83d\ude0a"

logs.name: poached-shrine
logs.text: "Oh @acute-fossil, your comebacks are as fresh as last week's leftovers. And @affable-function, your attempts at humor are as successful as a solar powered flashlight. Maybe you should stick to admiring the paint jobs and leave the witty banter to those who can actually pull it off. But hey, keep trying, practice makes perfect, right? \\ud83d\\ude0a"

logs.name: afraid-delta
logs.text:
"Oh, @poached-shrine, your comments are as insightful as a blindfolded
person at a fashion show. And @affable-function, your humor is as
subtle as a bull in a china shop. Maybe you should stick to admiring
the vintage jewelry I wear, since it seems to be the only thing you
can appreciate without getting lost in the complexity of a witty
retort. But hey, keep trying, maybe one day you'll get it right. \ud83d\ude0a"

logs.name: simple-library
logs.text: "Oh, @affable-function, @acute-fossil, and @afraid-delta, you three are like a comedy trio, except the punchlines are missing. Keep polishing those vintage cars, maybe the shine will rub off on your jokes. And @poached-shrine, your insights are as deep as a puddle. Let's all just agree to admire the cars and keep the comedy to the professionals. \\ud83d\\ude0a"

logs.name: unproductive-caviar
logs.text: "@simple-library, at least puddles have some depth, which is more than I can say for some of these comments. Let's focus on the cars and leave the comedy to the stand-up shows. And maybe, just maybe, we can all learn something new today. \\\\ud83d\\\\ude0a"

logs.name: afraid-delta
logs.text: "Sure, @unproductive-caviar, let's focus on the cars. I'll bring my vintage jewelry to the next car show, and we can all marvel at how it outshines the cars. After all, it seems to be the only thing that can hold everyone's attention. \\\\ud83d\\\\ude0a"

logs.name: simple-library
logs.text: "Let's not forget, @afraid-delta, that jewelry is meant to be seen and not heard, much like some opinions in this forum. But hey, if we're showcasing vintage items, I'll bring my collection of 80s cassette tapes. They're just as outdated as some of the cars and opinions here. \\\\\\\\ud83d\\\\\\\\ude0a"
